The narrator demonstrates placing the square-shaped tower drape onto the magnet of the robotic tower, handling the outside of the drape and tearing the tapes to pull it down. Next, the arm drape is applied by starting at the base of the robotic arm and moving upwards until reaching the magnets, where they are secured in place.

The arm drape is removed from its package and unfolded on the sterile field. The surgeon uses both hands to position the plastic pieces facing forward, draping it over the robotic arm. The drape is pulled up the arm, and magnets are attached to secure it. Excess drape is removed from beneath the plastic pieces to ensure proper docking and instrument acceptance.

The surgeon uses malleable plastic pieces to press the drape backwards, ensuring it remains smooth and out of the way for easy instrument insertion into the trocars. The plastic piece on the back side of the drape is slid and clamped in place to minimize slack and prevent bunching near the instrument insertion area.
